opp.name: Civeo _ S2C_May26
opp.next_step: Abeerah: Reach out to Scott and set up a demo call post conversation with Scott and Mike at ISM Denver
opp.ais_why:
* Scott (the executive sponsor) reinitiated a stalled 2024 procurement project after meeting Zycus at ISM Denver; an intro/demo call is being set up for June.
* The documented need is conventional S2C: "improve sourcing, supplier management, contract visibility, approvals, and spend visibility," with "cost optimization... due to revenue pressure" - no AI requirement, and scope (iContract/iSource/iSupplier) carries no AI modules.
* The buying agenda is efficiency and governance - "supplier oversight and procurement governance need stronger structure" - with budget still to be established ("Budget - No"); nothing points to AI as a driver.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 18 Jun 2026
Zero Avoma calls discovered
SF synthesis-map fields query returned no data
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case exists anywhere in the record. Salesforce synthesis-map fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c) returned no results or are unpopulated in this org. No Avoma calls exist to surface buyer-stated pain or quantified impact (cost reduction, cycle-time improvement, savings target). The Next Step references a demo post-ISM Denver, implying an initial conversation occurred, but that conversation was never recorded or logged, so the business driver remains unknown. Without a single discovery artifact the deal cannot be defended. The immediate move is to conduct a discovery call with Scott Tymchuk and map the operational pain, current-state cost/inefficiency, and target metrics (savings %, cycle time, manual effort reduction) before any demo.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Scott Tymchuk, Decision Maker
SF champion synthesis-map fields query returned no data
Zero Avoma calls
Engagement pulse: DARK, no verified buyer engagement
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed. The single contact role is Scott Tymchuk, Director Supply Chain, marked Decision Maker, but there is no evidence of advocacy, internal selling, or access to power. Salesforce champion fields (X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c) returned no results or are blank. Zero Avoma calls means we have never heard Scott sell Zycus internally, share competitive intelligence, introduce us to the Economic Buyer, or push the process forward. The Next Step indicates the rep plans to reach out to Scott for a demo, but that outreach has not occurred (no activity logged), so we do not know if Scott is engaged, passive, or unresponsive. The engagement pulse confirms the deal is dark. Without a champion who can navigate the buying committee, provide competitive intel, and advocate when we are not in the room, this deal cannot progress in a large enterprise environment. The move is to re-engage Scott, run a discovery call, and assess his willingness to champion Zycus: Does he have a strong opinion that we are the right fit? Will he introduce us to finance/procurement/IT stakeholders? Will he share what other vendors Civeo is considering? If Scott is passive or unresponsive, we need to multi-thread immediately and find an active internal advocate.

sources: SF Competitors__c null
SF competition synthesis-map fields query returned no data
Zero Avoma calls
competition.narrative: No competitive intelligence exists. Salesforce Competitors__c is null, and the synthesis-map competition fields (Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Moved_To__c, Why_not_Zycus__c, Closed_Lost_Reason_Code__c) returned no results or are blank. Zero Avoma calls means no buyer has mentioned another vendor, no rep has asked about the current stack or alternatives being evaluated, and no competitive positioning has occurred. We do not know if this is a sole-source opportunity, a competitive RFP with Coupa/SAP Ariba/Jaggaer/GEP in the mix, or a replacement of an incumbent system. The Products scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier) suggests a Source-to-Contract play, where SAP Ariba, Coupa, and Ivalua are the most common enterprise competitors, but that is market inference, not deal-specific intelligence. The move is to surface competition on the discovery call with Scott: How are you handling procurement/sourcing today (manual, incumbent system, spreadsheets)? Are you evaluating other vendors? What alternatives have you looked at or been pitched? If this is a competitive process, what are the top 2-3 reasons you would choose Zycus over the alternatives? Without this intelligence we cannot position our differentiation (Merlin AI negotiation, supplier collaboration depth, contract compliance automation) or build a wedge strategy.

sources: SF pain synthesis-map fields query returned no data
SF Next_Step__c 18 Jun 2026 references ISM Denver conversation, but no recording or task capturing that discussion
Zero Avoma calls
identify_pain.narrative: No operational or financial pain has been articulated by the buyer. Salesforce pain-related fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c) returned no results or are unpopulated. Zero Avoma calls means no buyer has stated on the record what is broken, what the cost of inaction is, or what business outcome they need. The Next Step references reaching out post-ISM Denver, implying an initial conversation happened at the ISM conference, but that conversation was never recorded or captured in Salesforce, so the pain remains invisible. The Products scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier) suggests procurement/sourcing inefficiency, but that is inference, not confirmed pain. We do not know if Civeo is replacing an incumbent system (Existing_vendor__c is blank), addressing manual processes, or solving a compliance/visibility gap. Without confirmed pain the deal cannot be prioritized or defended. The move is to lead the discovery call with open-ended pain questions: What procurement/sourcing challenges are you facing today? What is the cost (time, money, risk) of your current approach? What happens if this is not fixed? Who owns this pain internally?

sources: SF paper-process synthesis-map fields query returned no data
SF Current_Contract_Expiration__c not populated
Zero Avoma calls
paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, or procurement mechanics have been discussed. Salesforce paper-process related fields returned no results or are blank, and zero Avoma calls means no conversation about MSA/SOW, legal review, InfoSec requirements, ERP integration, or SI/partner involvement. We do not know if Civeo requires a Master Service Agreement, whether they have standard terms, who their legal/procurement contacts are, or if an implementation partner (SI) is involved. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is not populated, so there is no forcing function from an incumbent contract rolling off. For a Source-to-Contract SaaS deal of this size, InfoSec review, data-privacy terms, and integration with their ERP (likely SAP or Oracle given the industry) will be required, but none of that has been scoped. The move is to surface paper-process requirements on the discovery call: ask Scott who handles vendor contracts, whether Civeo has a preferred MSA, and what InfoSec/integration review will be required before contracting can begin.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Scott Tymchuk, Decision Maker, no EB role present
Zero Avoma calls
SF EB synthesis-map fields query returned no data
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is identified in any source. The single contact role is Scott Tymchuk, Director Supply Chain, marked Decision Maker, but there is no evidence of his budget authority, no conversation captured about commercial approval, and no CFO/VP-level sponsor mapped. Salesforce EB-related fields (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c) returned no results or are unfilled. Zero Avoma calls means no one has discussed pricing, approval chains, or budget ownership on the record. For a $100K multi-product SaaS deal (iContract, iSource, iSupplier) in a publicly traded energy-services company, budget authority almost certainly sits above Director level. The gap is total: we do not know who controls the commercial decision, whether Scott can sign, or who the CFO/procurement sponsor is. The move is to ask Scott directly on the discovery call who approves the budget and request an introduction to that person before commercials begin.

sources: SF decision-process synthesis-map fields query returned no data
Zero Avoma calls
Engagement pulse: DARK, no verified activity
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is completely unknown. Salesforce process fields (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c) returned no results or are unpopulated. Zero Avoma calls means no discussion of RFI/RFP rounds, vendor shortlisting, approval chains, or timeline milestones. We do not know if this is a competitive bid, a sole-source evaluation, or an exploratory conversation. We do not know if Civeo has issued an RFP, who else is in the mix, or what their internal approval process requires (procurement, legal, InfoSec, CFO sign-off). The single contact role (Scott, Decision Maker) suggests he may own the process, but without a conversation we cannot confirm his authority or the governance structure. The engagement pulse shows this deal is dark, so the process may not have started, or it may have stalled. The move is to reopen the relationship with Scott, confirm whether a formal evaluation is underway, map the decision committee (procurement, finance, IT, legal), and establish the milestone dates (RFI due, shortlist, selection, contracting).

sources: SF Products__c: iContract;iSource;iSupplier
SF decision-criteria synthesis-map fields query returned no data
Zero Avoma calls
decision_criteria.narrative: No decision criteria, RFP scope, or evaluation framework has been documented. Salesforce criteria fields (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c) returned no results or are blank. Zero Avoma calls means no use-case validation, test-case discussion, or workshop feedback exists. The Products field lists iContract, iSource, iSupplier, indicating a Source-to-Contract scope, but we have no evidence of what Civeo's procurement challenges are, what their current tooling gaps are, or how they will evaluate vendors. The Next Step implies a demo is the next milestone, but running a demo without documented criteria is guesswork. The correct sequence is discovery first (map pain and requirements), then a tailored demo against those requirements. The move is to hold the demo until a discovery call surfaces Civeo's top 3-5 evaluation priorities (e.g. supplier onboarding speed, contract compliance, spend visibility, AI-driven negotiation automation) and confirm whether this is an RFI/RFP process or a sole-source evaluation.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — No AI fit signal exists. AIS fields are null, and zero Avoma calls means no buyer has discussed AI needs, automation priorities, or appetite for agentic workflows. The Products scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier) includes categories where Merlin AI adds high value (contract negotiation intelligence, sourcing automation), but we have no evidence of buyer interest or readiness.
bullets.text: Losing on capability fit — No AI fit signal exists.

bullets.full: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — AI positioning strength is unknown. AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, and AIS_Why__c are all null, so there is no signal of AI appetite, Merlin positioning, or agentic-automation interest. Zero Avoma calls means no conversation about AI-driven negotiation, intelligent contract management, or autonomous sourcing workflows has occurred. For a Source-to-Contract scope where Merlin (AI negotiation agent on supplier price history, contract intelligence) is a core differentiator, this is a critical positioning gap. Without understanding Civeo's AI maturity or appetite, we cannot tailor the narrative or prioritize Merlin in the demo.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 0 reflects a weak position: momentum 46 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: momentum 46 vs stage-expected 50.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 46 (roughly flat): Engagement cadence dropped (pulse dark). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, commercial). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 37 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 1 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (0), nudged by commitment 37 and momentum 46, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole, SF Next_Step__c 18 Jun 2026, engagement pulse DARK
champion_strength.summary: No champion exists. Scott Tymchuk is the single contact role (Decision Maker), but there is zero evidence of advocacy, engagement, or internal selling. The Next Step indicates a planned demo outreach to Scott post-ISM Denver (18 Jun), but that outreach never occurred and no buyer response is logged. The deal is dark.

items.commitment: Reach out to Scott Tymchuk and schedule demo call (post-ISM Denver conversation)

items.action: Re-engage Scott Tymchuk immediately: phone + email blitz to confirm ISM Denver conversation recap, assess his interest level, and schedule a discovery call (not demo yet) to map pain, metrics, decision process, Economic Buyer, and competitive landscape before any product presentation.
items.trigger: Deal is completely dark: LastActivityDate null, Next Step from 18 Jun references planned outreach to Scott that never occurred, zero buyer engagement for 50 days, and 167 days to a December close that is indefensible without discovery.
items.expected_effect: Restart the relationship, confirm buyer interest, and establish whether this is a live opportunity or a dead lead. If Scott is responsive, book discovery within 7 days. If unresponsive after 3 touch attempts over 5 days, escalate to manager (Michael McCarthy) for executive-level outreach or mark the deal for disqualification.

items.action: Run a full discovery call (30-45 min) with Scott before any demo: map operational pain (manual sourcing, contract compliance gaps, spend visibility issues), quantify impact (cost, cycle time, error rate), identify Economic Buyer and budget approval process, surface decision criteria and competitive alternatives, and assess AI appetite for Merlin positioning.
items.trigger: Zero MEDDPICC coverage: no pain identified, no metrics, no Economic Buyer, no decision criteria, no decision process, no champion development, no competitive intelligence. The deal cannot be defended or progressed without discovery depth.
items.expected_effect: Build the foundation for a qualified opportunity: confirm business case, map stakeholders and decision structure, surface competitive positioning requirements, and determine whether the December close date is realistic or needs to slip. Discovery is the gate to all subsequent milestones (demo, RFI/RFP, commercials).

items.action: Multi-thread immediately: ask Scott on the discovery call to introduce the deal team to procurement lead, IT/integration stakeholder, and the finance/CFO sponsor who will approve the $100K budget. Map the buying committee (5-7 people typical for this deal size) and establish contact with at least 2 additional influencers within 2 weeks.
items.trigger: Single-threaded to Scott Tymchuk (Decision Maker) with zero engagement evidence. Only 1 contact role on the opp. Extreme single-thread risk in a large enterprise environment where procurement, finance, IT, and legal all have a voice.
items.expected_effect: Reduce single-thread risk, gain visibility into the full decision committee, and identify the Economic Buyer and potential champion. Multi-threading is required to survive the enterprise buying motion and defend the forecast.

items.action: Surface competitive intelligence on the discovery call: ask Scott how Civeo is handling procurement/sourcing today (manual, incumbent system, spreadsheets), whether they are evaluating other vendors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, Jaggaer), and what alternatives they have been pitched. If competitive, build a wedge strategy around Merlin AI negotiation, supplier collaboration depth, and contract compliance automation.
items.trigger: Zero competitive intelligence: Competitors__c null, no synthesis-map competition data, no Avoma calls. We do not know if this is sole-source, competitive RFP, or replacement deal, and cannot position differentiation without this.
items.expected_effect: Understand the competitive landscape, tailor positioning to beat the alternatives, and build a wedge strategy. If sole-source, confirm why Zycus is the only option and lock in champion advocacy. If competitive, identify the top 2 threats and build a differentiation narrative (Merlin AI for negotiation intelligence is the strongest wedge against Coupa/Ariba commodity plays).

items.action: Assess and reset the close date: after discovery, evaluate whether a December 2026 close is realistic given current state (50 days in Qualified, zero discovery, no Economic Buyer, no decision process). If the buyer's timeline is different or this is early-stage exploration, update the close date in Salesforce to reflect reality and reset the forecast to Omitted or Pipeline (not Best Case/Commit).
items.trigger: Deal is 167 days from close but still in Qualified stage with zero progression indicators. The timeline is indefensible: a 12-15 month enterprise motion starting today would close in mid-2027, not December 2026.
items.expected_effect: Align the deal timeline with reality, prevent forecast embarrassment, and set honest expectations with leadership. If the buyer has a genuine December forcing function (budget year-end, contract expiration), discovery will surface it and the deal can be accelerated with executive sponsorship. If not, slip the close date to Q2 2027 and work the proper enterprise motion.

confidence_signals.summary: Zero confidence signals exist. LastActivityDate is null, no Avoma calls, no completed tasks, no buyer engagement since creation 50 days ago. The Next Step references a planned demo outreach (18 Jun), but that outreach never occurred. The deal is completely dark.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 15 May 2026, still in Qualified stage 50 days later with zero progression. Close date 18 Dec 2026 is 167 days out. Zero forward movement: LastActivityDate is null, no completed tasks, no buyer calls, no field updates beyond Next Step on 18 Jun. Time-in-Qualified is 50 days with no evidence of discovery depth, RFI/RFP positioning, or advancement signal. The rep's Next Step references a conversation at ISM Denver and reaching out to Scott for a demo, but there is no record of that outreach occurring, no demo scheduled, and no buyer response. Required pace for a 12-15 month enterprise motion would place this deal in discovery depth by now; it has not started.

north_star_verdict.headline: 167 days to close, zero buyer engagement since creation, no discovery, single-threaded, completely dark

flags: Re-engage immediately with Scott Tymchuk (the sole Decision Maker contact) to run a full discovery call. This deal has been silent for 11+ months with zero recorded buyer engagement. The Next_Step references reaching out to Scott post ISM Denver, but there is no evidence ISM Denver occurred or that the demo was scheduled. Without a live conversation, this deal is a ghost. The discovery call must uncover the business problem, quantify the pain, map the buying committee (EB, DM, Champion, influencers), understand the competitive landscape (are they evaluating other vendors, replacing an incumbent, running an RFP), and validate the decision process and timeline. If Scott does not respond within 10 business days, escalate to the deal owner's manager for an executive-level re-engagement attempt.

Multi-thread beyond Scott Tymchuk. The opportunity has only 1 buyer-side contact role, but the account has 18+ contacts including procurement leads (Darren Ehman, Nathaniel Cooper), supply chain managers (Joshua Ceulemans, Donna Suess, Brian Dunsire), IT leadership (Charles Lab CIO, Doug Jacoby CISO, Joel MacLean Sr. Director Business Applications), finance (Barclay Brewer Interim CFO, Lisa Hopner Corporate Controller), and C-level (Bradley Dodson CEO). Zero engagement with any of these means single-thread risk is extreme. On the discovery call, ask Scott who else is involved in the P2P/S2C evaluation (procurement, IT, finance, legal) and secure introductions to at least 2-3 other stakeholders, prioritizing the Economic Buyer and a potential Champion.

Validate or disqualify by 15 July 2026. This deal is 42 days old, still in Qualified, and has been silent for 11+ months. If the re-engagement attempt yields no response or reveals Civeo is not actively evaluating P2P/S2C transformation, disqualify the opportunity rather than carry a ghost deal in the forecast. A $100k deal with a Dec 2026 close date sitting in Qualified with zero buyer engagement and zero discovery depth is not a real pipeline deal. The move is to force a binary outcome within 3 weeks: either restart the deal with a live discovery call and forward momentum, or close it as unqualified.
Single-threaded to Scott Tymchuk (Decision Maker) with zero engagement evidence. No multi-threading, no influencer map, no Economic Buyer contact. Extreme single-thread risk.

Zero MEDDPICC coverage: no pain identified, no metrics, no Economic Buyer, no decision criteria, no decision process, no champion, no competitive intelligence. The deal is a placeholder, not a qualified opportunity.

Completely dark: LastActivityDate is null, no Avoma calls, no completed tasks, no buyer engagement since creation 50 days ago. The Next Step references a planned demo outreach post-ISM Denver (18 Jun), but that outreach never occurred.

Timeline indefensible: 167 days to close (18 Dec 2026) but still in Qualified stage with zero discovery depth, no RFI/RFP positioning, no advancement signal. Required pace for a 12-15 month enterprise motion would place this deal in Formal Evaluation by now.

No AIS signal: AIS fields are null, so AI appetite and Merlin positioning strength are unknown. For a Source-to-Contract scope where AI-driven negotiation (Merlin) is a key differentiator, this is a positioning gap.

No competitive intelligence: Competitors__c is null, zero calls, no synthesis-map competition data. We do not know if this is sole-source, competitive RFP, or replacement, and cannot position differentiation.
No champion: Scott Tymchuk is marked Decision Maker but has zero engagement evidence and no indication of advocacy, internal selling, or access to the Economic Buyer.

No use-case validation, test case, or workshop has occurred.

hard.ais_why:
* Scott (the executive sponsor) reinitiated a stalled 2024 procurement project after meeting Zycus at ISM Denver; an intro/demo call is being set up for June.
* The documented need is conventional S2C: "improve sourcing, supplier management, contract visibility, approvals, and spend visibility," with "cost optimization... due to revenue pressure" - no AI requirement, and scope (iContract/iSource/iSupplier) carries no AI modules.
* The buying agenda is efficiency and governance - "supplier oversight and procurement governance need stronger structure" - with budget still to be established ("Budget - No"); nothing points to AI as a driver.
hard.next_step:
Abeerah:

  • Reach out to Scott and set up a demo call post conversation with Scott and Mike at ISM Denver

deltas.to: No champion: Scott Tymchuk is marked Decision Maker but has zero engagement evidence and no indication of advocacy, internal selling, or access to the Economic
deltas.subject: No champion: Scott Tymchuk is marked Decision Maker but has zero engagement evidence and no indication of advocacy, internal selling, or access to the Economic Buyer.

Deal is 175 days from close (18 Dec 2026) but still in Qualified stage with zero buyer engagement, zero discovery depth, no Economic Buyer, no champion, no competitive intelligence, and no decision process mapped. The timeline is indefensible.
Deal has been silent for 11+ months. Two scheduled touchpoints in June and July 2025 had no recordings (one cancelled, one not recorded). Zero forward movement since. LastActivityDate is null.

Re-engage immediately with Scott Tymchuk (the sole Decision Maker contact) to run a full discovery call. This deal has been silent for 11+ months with zero recorded buyer engagement. The Next_Step references reaching out to Scott post ISM Denver, but there is no evidence ISM Denver occurred or that the demo was scheduled. Without a live conversation, this deal is a ghost. The discovery call must uncover the business problem, quantify the pain, map the buying committee (EB, DM, Champion, influencers), understand the competitive landscape (are they evaluating other vendors, replacing an incumbent, running an RFP), and validate the decision process and timeline. If Scott does not respond within 10 business days, escalate to the deal owner's manager for an executive-level re-engagement attempt.

Multi-thread beyond Scott Tymchuk. The opportunity has only 1 buyer-side contact role, but the account has 18+ contacts including procurement leads (Darren Ehman, Nathaniel Cooper), supply chain managers (Joshua Ceulemans, Donna Suess, Brian Dunsire), IT leadership (Charles Lab CIO, Doug Jacoby CISO, Joel MacLean Sr. Director Business Applications), finance (Barclay Brewer Interim CFO, Lisa Hopner Corporate Controller), and C-level (Bradley Dodson CEO). Zero engagement with any of these means single-thread risk is extreme. On the discovery call, ask Scott who else is involved in the P2P/S2C evaluation (procurement, IT, finance, legal) and secure introductions to at least 2-3 other stakeholders, prioritizing the Economic Buyer and a potential Champion.

Validate or disqualify by 15 July 2026. This deal is 42 days old, still in Qualified, and has been silent for 11+ months. If the re-engagement attempt yields no response or reveals Civeo is not actively evaluating P2P/S2C transformation, disqualify the opportunity rather than carry a ghost deal in the forecast. A $100k deal with a Dec 2026 close date sitting in Qualified with zero buyer engagement and zero discovery depth is not a real pipeline deal. The move is to force a binary outcome within 3 weeks: either restart the deal with a live discovery call and forward momentum, or close it as unqualified.

Salesforce synthesis-map fields for MEDDPICC and competition (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Competitors__c, X10a_Sponsor__c, X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, What_is_the_decision_process__c, and 20+ related fields) returned no results or are unpopulated, so no fallback Salesforce source exists for discovery depth.
